pattern: Stitching small BSTs by matching leaf values to root values, then validating that the single assembled tree obeys the global ordering with one in-order sweep.
hints: Each root value is unique, so a root that never appears as a leaf in any tree is the only candidate to end up on top.
Index the pieces by root value and graft a piece onto a matching leaf exactly once, then make sure every piece got consumed and no leaf demands a missing root.

Splicing alone is not enough: walk the assembled tree once checking the strict BST property (left subtree bounded above by the node, right subtree bounded below), and confirm the node count equals the total of all pieces.

edge_inputs.description: three pieces chain together into a valid BST
only one merge possible but the result breaks the BST order
no leaf matches any root so nothing can merge
a single piece is already the whole tree
single piece with two children, nothing to merge
two pieces but a leaf points to a root that is not present
a cycle of leaf-root references leaves a piece unconsumed
left-side graft producing a valid larger tree

stress.description: construct a genuine valid BST then shatter it into <=3-node pieces, occasionally perturbing to make it invalid
stress.note: starts from a real BST so a large fraction of cases are solvable; perturbation injects impossible cases
